info.title: Daffa API
info.description: Generated from the route table in internal/api/server.go — the table IS the authorization rule, and every operation names its capability and where it is checked. Authenticate with a session cookie or a personal API token (see docs/tokens.md).

get.description: The masks in caps/caps_by_env are one number per functional area; the browser resolves a question as `global | byEnv[currentHost]` (see hasCap in caps.ts).

get.summary: List your own API tokens
get.description: Self-service is open for the same reason /me is: managing your own credential is a property of being signed in, not a granted power. Token-authenticated callers are refused in the handlers — a token that could mint tokens would survive its own revocation. See docs/tokens.md.
get.x-daffa-open: your own credentials are yours to see; the response carries names and prefixes, never a secret

post.summary: Mint an API token for yourself; the secret is returned exactly once
post.description: The response is the only time the secret ever exists outside the caller's hands: only its hash is stored, so there is no second chance to read it.
post.x-daffa-open: minting a credential for yourself is not a granted power; the handler refuses token-authenticated callers

get.summary: Open an interactive shell in a container (WebSocket, not JSON)
get.description:
Exec checks containers.exec INSIDE the handler, not here: a WebSocket upgrade is a GET, so it cannot be distinguished from a read by the route pattern. It is open at this layer and guarded at the next. See handleExec.

The wire protocol is a WebSocket, not JSON: binary frames are raw terminal bytes in both directions, text frames are control messages (resize). It cannot be described as a JSON operation, so it is documented here in prose only.
get.x-daffa-open: a WebSocket upgrade is a GET; handleExec enforces containers.exec itself

The node table is the exception that proves it — it is a JOIN of what the swarm says exists against what Daffa can reach, so it needs both, and it is gated on clusters.view because a node is what a cluster is MADE of rather than a resource of its own.

patch.description:
Node operations are their own capability. Draining a machine moves EVERYBODY's workload; scaling one service moves one. An operator trusted with the second has not thereby been trusted with the first. Say what to change: an availability (active|pause|drain) or a role (manager|worker) — one per request.

post.summary: Turn a standalone host into a single-node Swarm
post.description:
Swarm secrets and configs are retired: a secret is a STACK's sealed sub-resource now (GET/PUT /api/stacks/{id}/secrets, docs/secrets.md), and config lives in git behind a volume source (docs/volumes.md). The cluster's own existence. The join tokens are a CREDENTIAL — anybody holding one can add a machine to the cluster — so they are served by exactly one route, which requires swarm.edit, and they appear in no other payload anywhere.

Requires the capability at the host named in the path.

403.description: Signed in, but the capability is not held at the required scope.

500.description: Something went wrong on the server; the audit log has the details.

get.summary: List audit entries, newest first
get.description:
Its own capability. Who tried what, and was refused, is exactly the sort of thing you do not hand to everyone with a login by default. Entries with no host — users, roles, identity providers — are shown only to a GLOBAL audit.view holder.

Satisfied by the capability held globally or on any host; list responses are filtered to what the caller may see.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: at most this many entries (default 200, max 500)

403.description: Signed in, but the capability is not held at the required scope.

500.description: Something went wrong on the server; the audit log has the details.

session.name: __Host-daffa
session.description: Browser session cookie, established by POST /api/auth/login (daffa_session when served over plain HTTP, where __Host- cannot be set). CSRF-protected; browsers only.

token.description: Personal API token (Authorization: Bearer daffa_…), minted under your account. Some self-service routes refuse token callers; their descriptions say so.

message.description: Human-readable sentence, written for the operator.
